title: Prevent Classes from Accessing their Own Subclasses During Class Initialization
description: Avoid referencing a static member of a subclass from its parent during class initialization. This can make the code more fragile and prone to future bugs due to heavy reliance on the order of initialization of classes and their static members. This could create an initialization cycle or even a deadlock in extreme cases. If the order of the static class members changes, it might alter the behavior of the program, and these issues can be difficult to diagnose.
recommendation: Avoid creating dependencies between subclasses and their parents during class initialization. Do not reference static members of a subclass from its parent class during the initialization process.
impact: Not adhering to this rule can result in initialization cycles or deadlocks, and alterations to the order of static class members can modify the program behavior. These issues can be challenging to diagnose and fix, causing significant delays in development.

checks.message: Rule: Prevent Classes from Accessing their Own Subclasses During Class Initialization
